name: Policy Cycle
summary: A memory tool for turning issues into policy stages: issue, options, consultation, decision, delivery, evaluation.
whenToUseIt: Use this to structure policy analysis, briefs, stakeholder planning, or post-mortems on failed reforms.
steps: State the issue and how it is currently framed.
List the plausible options and tradeoffs.
Identify who must be consulted and why.
Mark the real decision point: minister, Cabinet, agency, or Parliament.
Define the delivery path and how success will be evaluated.
example: A public housing reform can be tracked from problem framing through consultation with states and industry, Cabinet choice, budget or law, then implementation and review.
